behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in heuristic quality logic itself (route construction/costing), not OS/network/subprocess or AI output. A deterministic in-process test can feed fixed coordinate sets with known optimal lengths and assert ratio <= 1.5. Higher levels were considered and rejected: behavior text is only about heuristic output quality, with no geocoding/API/runtime boundary requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run optimizer function/object directly on a corpus of fixed <=20-stop coordinate fixtures with precomputed optimal lengths; assert produced route length / optimal length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic math/config logic (distance-to-duration conversion per road type). Real infra is unnecessary; mocks do not hide the core failure because no external dependency behavior is under test. Higher levels rejected: behavior text specifies formula inputs (distance, configured speed), not network/OS/model effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call time-estimation function with table-driven cases covering multiple road types, speeds, and distances (including rounding boundaries); assert exact/expected windows per formula.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Pure validation rule on numeric totals and threshold comparison. No module handoff is required to trigger the failure, and no real infra/AI dependency exists. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is strictly validator behavior (reject + excess), which is local business logic.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with route-time and remaining-shift inputs around boundary conditions (equal, just over, far over); assert rejection status and exact excess duration.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is orchestration across modules: geocoding result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. A unit test of any single function can miss handoff bugs. System was considered but rejected because behavior text fixes API outcome as 'returns no results'; this can be reproduced with an in-process stub without real HTTP/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise service layer with a stub geocoder that returns coordinates for N-1 addresses and empty result for one; assert optimized route excludes failed stop and response includes that address in skipped list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Minimum realistic failure appears at module boundary: deduplication/aggregation must propagate correctly into route planning so the stop is visited once. Unit tests on dedupe logic alone can miss pipeline wiring regressions. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no AI/ML dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service flow in-process with duplicate addresses carrying different items; assert output has one stop for that address, aggregated items, and route contains that coordinate once.
